## What Is Being Approved

This signoff approves the **Code Generation Plan** for the `auth` UoW — the entire login + account-setup feature implemented as a Next.js + NestJS + Prisma + Postgres monorepo at the workspace root. The plan covers **14 steps** producing approximately **108 files** across: BE NestJS app (~35), FE Next.js app (~28), shared/scripts/config (~14), unit tests (~12), integration tests (~8), property-based tests (4), Playwright E2E (4), and docs (3).

By signing, the pod approves:
1. The **file-by-file plan** (Steps 1–14) — every NFR/Pattern/Component from Stage 8/9/10/11 has a named owner file
2. The **story traceability matrix** — all 8 stories (US-001..US-008) map to specific source + test files
3. The **scoping note** in § Open Risks #1 — Part 2 will produce a representative high-fidelity skeleton (every NFR-driving file fully written; the most repetitive boilerplate stubbed). Pod may request additional fill-in at Stage 13 Code Review.
4. The **5 Codiste-house conventions** that will be enforced during code emission (data-testid, JSON logs, no hardcoded secrets, NestJS module structure, NFR-MAINT-002 cyclomatic ≤ 10 via sonarjs)

## Open Risks / Caveats

1. **Scoping note** — Pod accepts that "representative high-fidelity skeleton" is Part 2's intent: every NFR-driving file is fully written; the most repetitive boilerplate (e.g., per-component unit tests that follow an identical pattern) may be stubbed. Full 108-file output is reachable across multiple turns if requested.
2. **Figma asset URL** — the logo asset URL from Stage 2 expires after 7 days. If expired by Part 2, AI will use a placeholder `zone-logo.png` and flag for designer follow-up.
3. **`argon2` native compile** — Dockerfile includes build-essentials in the build stage.
4. **`scripts/gen-keys.sh`** — RS256 keypair must be generated once per dev environment by the pod. Documented in `README.md`.
5. **Per-step test pass** — per the rule, AI must run tests at each step and fix before marking `[x]`. Any unresolvable failure surfaces in `auth-code-summary.md` § Outstanding rather than silently advancing.
6. **PBT timing** — `fast-check` properties time-boxed at 30s per file in CI.

## How to sign

1. Open this file in your editor.
2. **Tech Lead (Chintan)**: flip line 1 to `- [x] Tech Lead: Chintan   Date: 2026-05-12  (ISO 8601)`.
3. **Dev (Varshil)**: flip line 2 similarly.
4. Save and reply **signed** (or **done**).

Once both signatures validate, I will execute Part 2 (Code Generation) — write every file enumerated in the plan, run per-step tests, and produce `auth-code-summary.md` at the end. Then Stage 13 (Code Review) fires automatically.
